title: Rosetta map (schema_version 3)
description: Canonical, language-neutral schema for the `schema_version: 3` map format. This repo (rosetta-maps) OWNS this schema: it is the single source of truth for the format that the maps here are stored in, and the rosetta-frida (TS/Zod) and rosetta-xposed (Kotlin) adapters are clients that must track it. CI validates every map against this file; editors can point at it for autocompletion. Changing the format means bumping this schema first, then updating the client adapters.

version.description: Human-readable versionName label / fuzzy fallback only. Must contain a non-whitespace character (a whitespace-only label is rejected); version_code is the authoritative selection key.

version_code.description: Full Android longVersionCode ((versionCodeMajor << 32) | versionCode), never masked. Capped at Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (2^53 - 1) so the Frida JS client can represent it exactly.

captured_at.description: The date the map was captured, as an ISO 8601 calendar date `YYYY-MM-DD` (format: date). v3 change: previously a free-form string.

signer_sha256.description: The SHA-256 of the signing certificate(s). EITHER a single bare lowercase 64-hex string OR a non-empty array of such strings (match-any: a real app may present several signing certs). Canonical on-disk form is bare lowercase hex, NO colon separators, exactly 64 hex chars — the runtime clients normalize the APP-presented hash, not this stored value, so this stays strict. v3 change: the array form is newly allowed (was string-only).

generated_from.description: Optional provenance binding the map to the exact signatures revision it was generated from (v3 addition). When present, `signatures_rev` is required.

signatures_rev.description: The git commit (short or full SHA-1, 7-40 lowercase hex chars) of the signatures the map was generated from.

status.description: Optional lifecycle status (v3 addition). Absent is treated as `active`. `superseded` requires `superseded_by`; `retracted` marks a withdrawn map.

superseded_by.description: Optional pointer (a `version_code`) to the map that supersedes this one (v3 addition). The semantic validator enforces that this is present iff `status == "superseded"`.

client_hints.description: Optional, advisory hints for specific clients. NOT used by the core resolver; a client MAY consult them. Keeping them in a sub-object keeps the top-level identity keys clean and signals they are non-authoritative.

tool.description: Provenance of these entries. The canonical, client-neutral vocabulary is `sigmatcher`, `hand-authored`, and `rosetta-runtime-discovered` (the runtime-discovery token is framework-neutral on purpose — do NOT use a framework-specific spelling like `rosetta-frida-runtime-discovered`). Free-form values are permitted for other tools.
